title: Thinking layer (lite) — intent + query normalisation with Haiku fallback
current_state: Inputs like 'no sauce in subway aghhh' or 'wdum no sauce' confuse Pi. Pi grabs the closest L3 hit (which may be a noisy session summary) and answers literally. No deliberation step exists between message arrival and main-model dispatch.
structural_problem: The main response model carries two burdens: understand the user AND craft a response. When the input is ambiguous or colloquial, both burdens fail together. Splitting comprehension from response generation (a thinking step) lets a cheap model handle the first burden well.
target_state: agent/thinking.py exports normalise(bubble, history) -> {intent, normalised_query, confidence}. Two-job version (intent + normalised_query). Provider chain: Groq llama-3.3-70b → Claude Haiku 4.5 → skip silently (raw input passes through). ALWAYS-ON for non-command bubbles (Ash decision). Direct commands (exit, help, mode switches, /exit, /clear) bypass since they are not thoughts. Recall + clarifier + memory-id extraction deferred to T-124-full (separate ticket once lite ships).
files_affected: pi_agent.py — process_input invokes thinking layer before dispatch
design_notes: Two-job prompt only (NOT five): 'User said: <text>. Last 3 turns: <history>. Output JSON: {intent: greeting|complaint|info|action|clarification|other, normalised_query: str (typo-corrected, references resolved), confidence: 0..1}'
Provider chain: Groq llama-3.3-70b primary; Claude Haiku 4.5 fallback (mirror T-092 compression pattern); silent skip on both-fail
Run IN PARALLEL with recall step (T-123) once bubble closes — both are I/O-bound on different LLMs, no contention
Skip thinking ONLY when: input.lower() in command list (exit/help/mode switches/clear), input is a tool result, input is empty. Long deliberate inputs STILL go through thinking — Ash override.
If confidence < 0.6, main model is told to ask one clarifying question (no separate clarifier-generation step in lite)
Output injected into main model context as: 'Thinking layer parsed: intent=<X>, normalised: <Y>, confidence=<Z>'
Latency budget: 500ms; cap output at 150 tokens
Failure mode: both providers fail → skip silently with track_silent('thinking.both_providers_failed'); raw input dispatched
Telemetry: count thinking calls per day; surface in /passive Skill 15 candidates if quota burns
success_criteria: 'no sauce in subway aghhh' → intent=complaint, normalised_query='User is upset their subway order had sauce despite no-sauce preference'
'wdym' alone → confidence<0.6, main model asks clarifier
Direct commands (exit, help, mode switches, /exit, /clear) bypass thinking — measure: latency unchanged
All other inputs (short OR long) DO go through thinking — Ash explicit preference
Groq 429 → Haiku takes over without user-visible error
Both providers fail → input dispatched raw, track_silent recorded
Recall (T-123) and thinking run in parallel — combined latency ≤ max(200ms, 500ms)
risk_notes: Cost risk: Groq is free-tier limited; every Telegram bubble pays the call. Mitigation: 200-char skip cutoff; daily count tracked via track_silent. Latency risk: 500ms compound on already-debounced bubble. Mitigation: keep prompt tight; cap output at 200 tokens.
migration_plan: 2. Unit tests with mocked Groq response
3. Wire into process_input — branch on intent
4. Test on the canonical bad inputs: 'aghhh', 'wdym', 'no sauce'
